title: Manipulating Citations with cffr
text: Citation File Format (CFF) (Druskat et al. 2021) (v1.2.0) plain text files human- machine-readable citation information software (datasets). Code developers can include repositories let others know correctly cite software. format becoming popular within software citation ecosystem. Recently GitHub, Zenodo Zotero included full support citation format (Druskat 2021). GitHub support special interest:  — Nat Friedman (@natfriedman) July 27, 2021 See Customize repository/CITATION files info.

what: Creating a CITATION.cff file for my R package
title: Manipulating Citations with cffr
text: cffr creating CITATION.cff file quite straightforward. just need run cff_write(): hood, cff_write() performs following tasks: extracts metadata using cff_create(). Writes CITATION.cff file using yaml::write_yaml(). Validates result using cff_validate(). Congratulations! Now full CITATION.cff file R package.
code: library(cffr)  cff_write()  # You are done!

title: Manipulating Citations with cffr
text: valid keys Citation File Format schema version 1.2.0 can displayed cff_schema_keys(): case, going add url, version repository. also overwrite title key. just need create list pass keys argument cff_create():
code: newobject <- cff_create(cff())  # For modifying your auto-generated object, run this line instead: # newoobject <- cff_create()  newobject #> cff-version: 1.2.0 #> message: If you use this software, please cite it using these metadata. #> title: My Research Software #> authors: #> - family-names: Doe #>   given-names: John #> preferred-citation: #>   type: manual #>   title: My Research Software #>   authors: #>   - family-names: Doe #>     given-names: John #>   year: '2022' cff_schema_keys() #>  [1] "cff-version"         "message"             "type"                #>  [4] "license"             "title"               "version"             #>  [7] "doi"                 "abstract"            "authors"             #> [10] "preferred-citation"  "repository"          "repository-artifact" #> [13] "repository-code"     "url"                 "date-released"       #> [16] "contact"             "keywords"            "references"          #> [19] "commit"              "identifiers"         "license-url" newkeys <- list(   "url" = "https://ropensci.org/",   "version" = "0.0.1",   "repository" = "https://github.com/user/repo",   # If the field is already present, it would be overridden   title = "Modifying a 'cff' object" )  modobject <- cff_create(newobject, keys = newkeys)  modobject #> cff-version: 1.2.0 #> message: If you use this software, please cite it using these metadata. #> title: Modifying a 'cff' object #> version: 0.0.1 #> authors: #> - family-names: Doe #>   given-names: John #> preferred-citation: #>   type: manual #>   title: My Research Software #>   authors: #>   - family-names: Doe #>     given-names: John #>   year: '2022' #> repository: https://github.com/user/repo #> url: https://ropensci.org/  # Validate against the schema  cff_validate(modobject) #>  #> cff_validate results----- #> Congratulations! This cff object is valid

text: cffr provides two functions parse person objects bibentry objects (See ?person ?bibentry). objects included utils package core part metadata R package. Following previous example, going add new author first. , need first extract current author package append parsed person: Now, may want add references data. cffr supports two types references: References created bibentry() References extracted packages using citation() following example, add two references, one type: Now process similar example person: append references (lists) add object:
code: # Valid person keys  cff_schema_definitions_person() #>  [1] "address"       "affiliation"   "alias"         "city"          #>  [5] "country"       "email"         "family-names"  "fax"           #>  [9] "given-names"   "name-particle" "name-suffix"   "orcid"         #> [13] "post-code"     "region"        "tel"           "website"  # Create the person  chiquito <- person("Gregorio",   "Sánchez Fernández",   email = "fake@email2.com",   comment = c(     alias = "Chiquito de la Calzada",     city = "Malaga",     country = "ES",     ORCID = "0000-0000-0000-0001"   ) )  chiquito #> [1] "Gregorio Sánchez Fernández <fake@email2.com> (Chiquito de la Calzada, Malaga, ES, <https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0001>)"  # Parse it chiquito_parsed <- cff_parse_person(chiquito) chiquito_parsed #> family-names: Sánchez Fernández #> given-names: Gregorio #> email: fake@email2.com #> alias: Chiquito de la Calzada #> city: Malaga #> country: ES #> orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0001   # Append to previous authors  # Needs to be append as a list newauthors <- c(modobject$authors, list(chiquito_parsed)) newauthors #> - family-names: Doe #>   given-names: John #> - family-names: Sánchez Fernández #>   given-names: Gregorio #>   email: fake@email2.com #>   alias: Chiquito de la Calzada #>   city: Malaga #>   country: ES #>   orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0001  newauthorobject <- cff_create(modobject, keys = list(authors = newauthors))  newauthorobject #> cff-version: 1.2.0 #> message: If you use this software, please cite it using these metadata. #> title: Modifying a 'cff' object #> version: 0.0.1 #> authors: #> - family-names: Doe #>   given-names: John #> - family-names: Sánchez Fernández #>   given-names: Gregorio #>   email: fake@email2.com #>   alias: Chiquito de la Calzada #>   city: Malaga #>   country: ES #>   orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0000-0000-0001 #> preferred-citation: #>   type: manual #>   title: My Research Software #>   authors: #>   - Auto parsed from another R package base_r <- cff_parse_citation(citation("base"))  base_r #> type: manual #> title: 'R: A Language and Environment for Statistical Computing' #> authors: #> - name: R Core Team #> location: #>   name: Vienna, Austria #> year: '2022' #> url: https://www.R-project.org/ #> institution: #>   name: R Foundation for Statistical Computing  # title: From R to CFF

title: From R to CFF
text: key extracted “Repository” field DESCRIPTION file. Usually, field auto-populated package hosted repo (like CRAN r-universe). packages without field DESCRIPTION (typical case -development package), cffr try search package default repositories specified options("repos"). case Bioconductor packages, identified “biocViews” present DESCRIPTION file. cffr detects package available CRAN, return canonical url form package (.e. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=jsonlite). Example Back summary.
code: # Installed package  inst <- cff_create("jsonlite")  cat(inst$repository) #> https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=jsonlite  # Demo file downloaded from the r-universe  runiv <- system.file("examples/DESCRIPTION_r_universe", package = "cffr") runiv_cff <- cff_create(runiv) #> Warning in citation(n$package, auto = TRUE): no date field in DESCRIPTION file #> of package 'desc' #> Warning in citation(n$package, auto = TRUE): no date field in DESCRIPTION file #> of package 'gert' #> Warning in citation(n$package, auto = TRUE): no date field in DESCRIPTION file #> of package 'jsonvalidate'  cat(runiv_cff$repository) #> https://ropensci.r-universe.dev  desc::desc(runiv)$get("Repository") #>                        Repository  #> "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev"  # For in development package  norepo <- system.file("examples/DESCRIPTION_basic", package = "cffr")  # No repo norepo_cff <- cff_create(norepo)  cat(norepo_cff[["repository"]])  # Change the name to a known package on CRAN: ggplot2  tmp <- tempfile("DESCRIPTION") file.copy(norepo, tmp) #> [1] TRUE   # Change name desc::desc_set("Package", "ggplot2", file = tmp) #> Type: Package #> Package: ggplot2 #> Title: A Basic Description #> Version: 0.1.6 #> Authors@R (parsed): #>     * Marc Basic <marcbasic@gmail.com> [aut, cre, cph] #> Description: A very basic description. Should parse without #>     problems. #> License: GPL-3 #> URL: https://github.com/basic/package, #>     https://basic.github.io/package #> BugReports: https://github.com/basic/package/issues #> Encoding: UTF-8 #> LazyData: true #> RoxygenNote: 6.0.1.9000  cat(cff_create(tmp)[["repository"]]) #> https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=ggplot2  # Show what happens if another repo is set  # Save original config orig_options <- options() getOption("repos") #>                                                        universe  #>                               "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev"  #>                                                            CRAN  #> "https://packagemanager.rstudio.com/all/__linux__/jammy/latest"  #>                                                        BioCsoft  #>                   "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/bioc"  #>                                                         BioCann  #>        "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/data/annotation"  #>                                                         BioCexp  #>        "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/data/experiment"  #>                                                        fallback  #>                                   "https://cloud.r-project.org"   # Set new repos options(repos = c(   tidyverse = "https://tidyverse.r-universe.dev",   CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org" ))  # Load again the library # Repos are evaluated on load unloadNamespace("cffr") library(cffr)   cat(cff_create(tmp)[["repository"]]) #> https://tidyverse.r-universe.dev/  # Now it is the tidyverse repo, due to our new config!  # Reset original config options(orig_options) getOption("repos") #>                                                        universe  #>                               "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev"  #>                                                            CRAN  #> "https://packagemanager.rstudio.com/all/__linux__/jammy/latest"  #>                                                        BioCsoft  #>                   "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/bioc"  #>                                                         BioCann  #>        "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/data/annotation"  #>                                                         BioCexp  #>        "https://bioconductor.org/packages/3.15/data/experiment"  #>                                                        fallback  #>                                   "https://cloud.r-project.org"

title: cffr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-01-04 Now cffr extracts also information package dependencies adds main citation dependencies references field, using citation(auto = TRUE). New dependencies parameter cff_create() cff_write(). improvements cff_parse_citation(): cff_parse_citation() extracts information authors, based fields provided DESCRIPTION file. cff_parse_citation() better job extracting information bibentry() /BibTeX mapping preferred-citation/references fields CFF. Add new functions working git pre-commit hooks : cff_git_hook_install() cff_git_hook_remove() New BibTeX functions: cff_extract_to_bibtex() cff_to_bibtex() cff_parse_person_bibtex() write_bib() Add new dependency: lifecycle.

title: cffr 0.0.2
text: cffr part now rOpenSci. Update docs README. Add fuzzy match keys parameter. New dataset: cran_to_spdx. Add DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5509766 Citation installed packages extracted using citation(). Auto-generating preferred-citation key DESCRIPTION. Rename cff_schema_definitions_reference() cff_schema_definitions_refs(). “repository” key supported. Added vignette: vignette("crosswalk", package = "cffr"). Add support Bioconductor packages. New function: cff_gha_update().
